title: TABI - Discover Japan
description: Your guide to the real Japan: travel, culture, food, hidden places, and the things worth bringing home.

items.title: Okutama Lake and Forest Day From Tokyo Without Overstretching
items.summary: Okutama gives Tokyo travelers a real forest-and-water day, but it asks for transport discipline. Choose one lake or forest focus, check weather, and leave a return buffer before evening.
items.content_text: Okutama gives Tokyo travelers a real forest-and-water day, but it asks for transport discipline. Choose one lake or forest focus, check weather, and leave a return buffer before evening.

items.title: Chichibu Temple Walk From Tokyo: A Slower Pilgrimage-Minded Day
items.summary: Chichibu is close enough for a long day from Tokyo, but its temple culture deserves a slower pace. Use the trip as a town-and-temple walk rather than a list of numbered stops.
items.content_text: Chichibu is close enough for a long day from Tokyo, but its temple culture deserves a slower pace. Use the trip as a town-and-temple walk rather than a list of numbered stops.

items.title: Minoo Waterfall From Osaka: A Forest Walk With Enough Time to Breathe
items.summary: Minoo is a practical forest break from Osaka because the walk, station access, and waterfall goal are easy to understand. The best plan keeps time for weather, crowds, and the return instead of treating the forest as a quick photo stop.
items.content_text: Minoo is a practical forest break from Osaka because the walk, station access, and waterfall goal are easy to understand. The best plan keeps time for weather, crowds, and the return instead of treating the forest as a quick photo stop.

items.title: Hinohara Forest Valley Day: Tokyo Nature Beyond the Usual City Plan
items.summary: Hinohara shows another side of Tokyo: valleys, forest roads, small communities, and mountain weather. It rewards travelers who plan transport windows carefully and keep the day slower than a central-city itinerary.
items.content_text: Hinohara shows another side of Tokyo: valleys, forest roads, small communities, and mountain weather. It rewards travelers who plan transport windows carefully and keep the day slower than a central-city itinerary.

items.title: Sarushima From Tokyo or Yokohama: A Small Island Day With Real Timing
items.summary: Sarushima is close enough to feel simple, but island days depend on ferry timing, weather, and what you want from the walk. Plan it as a compact coastal history day rather than an add-on after too much city travel.
items.content_text: Sarushima is close enough to feel simple, but island days depend on ferry timing, weather, and what you want from the walk. Plan it as a compact coastal history day rather than an add-on after too much city travel.

items.title: Ueno Station Ekiben and Park Day: Food Before Museums or a Northbound Train
items.summary: Ueno is useful when a museum day, park walk, or northbound rail plan needs a simple meal. Use the station as an anchor, then decide whether the ekiben belongs before the park, after the museum, or on the train.
items.content_text: Ueno is useful when a museum day, park walk, or northbound rail plan needs a simple meal. Use the station as an anchor, then decide whether the ekiben belongs before the park, after the museum, or on the train.

items.title: Ueno to Asakusa Casual Matcha Breaks for a Slow Tokyo Afternoon
items.summary: A matcha break between Ueno and Asakusa works best as a pause, not a destination race. Build it around walking distance, museum fatigue, weather, and whether the next stop needs quiet or energy.
items.content_text: A matcha break between Ueno and Asakusa works best as a pause, not a destination race. Build it around walking distance, museum fatigue, weather, and whether the next stop needs quiet or energy.

items.title: Tokyo Station Ekiben Before the Shinkansen: How to Choose Without Rushing
items.summary: Tokyo Station is one of Japan's most useful places to buy ekiben before a long rail leg. This guide turns the choice into a calm sequence: confirm the train, decide when to eat, choose a box that travels well, and leave enough time to reach the platform.
items.content_text: Tokyo Station is one of Japan's most useful places to buy ekiben before a long rail leg. This guide turns the choice into a calm sequence: confirm the train, decide when to eat, choose a box that travels well, and leave enough time to reach the platform.

items.title: Shin-Osaka Ekiben Transfer Lunch: A Practical Stop Between Trains
items.summary: Shin-Osaka is a practical food stop because Shinkansen, local rail, and Osaka access meet in one station. The best ekiben choice is not always the most famous one; it is the one that fits the transfer, luggage, and next ride.
items.content_text: Shin-Osaka is a practical food stop because Shinkansen, local rail, and Osaka access meet in one station. The best ekiben choice is not always the most famous one; it is the one that fits the transfer, luggage, and next ride.

items.title: Tokyo Drugstore Sunscreen and Skincare Basics for Travelers
items.summary: Tokyo drugstores can solve real travel problems: sun, humidity, dry hotel air, and small forgotten items. The safest approach is to buy by need and label clarity, not by shelf popularity.
items.content_text: Tokyo drugstores can solve real travel problems: sun, humidity, dry hotel air, and small forgotten items. The safest approach is to buy by need and label clarity, not by shelf popularity.

items.title: Osaka Drugstore Travel Kit: Practical Items Before a Long Day Out
items.summary: A drugstore stop before a long Osaka day can prevent small problems from shaping the itinerary. Think hydration, sun, blisters, masks, and hotel-room comfort before souvenir shopping.
items.content_text: A drugstore stop before a long Osaka day can prevent small problems from shaping the itinerary. Think hydration, sun, blisters, masks, and hotel-room comfort before souvenir shopping.

items.title: Ginza and Nihonbashi Matcha Shopping Without Guessing at Labels
items.summary: Ginza and Nihonbashi make tea shopping easy to pair with a Tokyo walking day. The practical question is not just ceremonial versus culinary; it is how soon you will use the tea, how you will store it, and whether you can ask the shop for the intended preparation.
items.content_text: Ginza and Nihonbashi make tea shopping easy to pair with a Tokyo walking day. The practical question is not just ceremonial versus culinary; it is how soon you will use the tea, how you will store it, and whether you can ask the shop for the intended preparation.

items.title: Osaka Department Store Matcha Sweets: Choosing Gifts That Travel Well
items.summary: Osaka department store food floors are useful for matcha sweets because packaging, shelf life, and gift presentation are easier to compare in one place. Choose by travel conditions first, then flavor.

items.title: Shinjuku Beauty Floors for First-Time Visitors Who Do Not Want to Overbuy
items.summary: Shinjuku's beauty floors are useful because many counters and drugstores sit close to major rail exits. Make a short list before entering, then compare texture, language support, and travel size rather than buying by display alone.
items.content_text: Shinjuku's beauty floors are useful because many counters and drugstores sit close to major rail exits. Make a short list before entering, then compare texture, language support, and travel size rather than buying by display alone.

items.title: Sakai Knife Shopping and Maintenance Questions Before You Buy
items.summary: Sakai's knife culture is a serious craft story, not just a souvenir stop. Before buying, ask about steel type, sharpening, transport, and whether the knife fits the cooking you actually do.
items.content_text: Sakai's knife culture is a serious craft story, not just a souvenir stop. Before buying, ask about steel type, sharpening, transport, and whether the knife fits the cooking you actually do.

items.title: Mt. Takao From Tokyo: A First Hike That Still Needs Real Planning
items.summary: Mt. Takao is accessible from Tokyo, but easy access does not remove the need for weather, footwear, route, and return-time planning. Treat it as a mountain day, not just a sightseeing shortcut.
items.content_text: Mt. Takao is accessible from Tokyo, but easy access does not remove the need for weather, footwear, route, and return-time planning. Treat it as a mountain day, not just a sightseeing shortcut.

items.title: Ginza Stationery and Paper Gifts: Small Things Worth Bringing Home
items.summary: Stationery works well as a Tokyo souvenir because it is light, useful, and often tied to craft or design. In Ginza, choose by how it will be used after the trip, not only by how it looks in the shop.
items.content_text: Stationery works well as a Tokyo souvenir because it is light, useful, and often tied to craft or design. In Ginza, choose by how it will be used after the trip, not only by how it looks in the shop.

items.title: Osaka Stationery in Umeda and Namba: Practical Paper, Pens, and Desk Gifts
items.summary: Umeda and Namba make stationery shopping easy to fold into a wider Osaka day. The strongest purchases are practical: notebooks that survive bags, pens you will actually use, and small paper goods that pack flat.
items.content_text: Umeda and Namba make stationery shopping easy to fold into a wider Osaka day. The strongest purchases are practical: notebooks that survive bags, pens you will actually use, and small paper goods that pack flat.

items.title: Sumiyoshi Taisha in Osaka: A Shrine Walk With Ritual Context
items.summary: Sumiyoshi Taisha is one of Osaka's most important shrine visits, but the value of the day is not only the bridge or the photo. Learn the basic shrine etiquette, give the grounds time, and check current event access before going.
items.content_text: Sumiyoshi Taisha is one of Osaka's most important shrine visits, but the value of the day is not only the bridge or the photo. Learn the basic shrine etiquette, give the grounds time, and check current event access before going.

items.title: Kamakura Temple Pilgrimage Day Without Turning It Into a Checklist
items.summary: Kamakura has enough temples and shrines to overwhelm a day. A stronger plan chooses a small route, respects worship spaces, checks current access, and leaves time for the town between sites.
items.content_text: Kamakura has enough temples and shrines to overwhelm a day. A stronger plan chooses a small route, respects worship spaces, checks current access, and leaves time for the town between sites.
